core.label: Rust Fundamentals
core.description: Installation, syntax, types, and ownership basics.

topics.label: Installation & Cargo
topics.description: Install Rust with rustup and manage projects with Cargo. Cargo handles building, testing, and dependencies.

topics.label: Variables & Mutability
topics.description: Variables are immutable by default — use mut for mutability. Immutability by default prevents accidental changes.

topics.label: Data Types
topics.description: Scalar types (i32, f64, bool, char) and compound types (tuples, arrays). Rust is statically typed with inference.

topics.label: Functions
topics.description: fn keyword, parameters with types, return values, and expressions vs statements. Functions are central to Rust.

topics.label: Control Flow
topics.description: if/else (as expressions), loop, while, for, match. if and match can return values.

topics.label: Strings (String vs &str)
topics.description: Owned String vs borrowed &str. Understanding this distinction is key to working with text in Rust.

topics.label: Collections (Vec, HashMap)
topics.description: Dynamic arrays with Vec and key-value maps with HashMap. The most used collection types.

topics.label: Shadowing & Constants
topics.description: Re-declare variables with let and define compile-time constants with const. Shadowing allows type changes.

core.label: Ownership & Borrowing
core.description: Rust's ownership system, borrowing, and lifetimes.

topics.label: Ownership Rules
topics.description: Each value has one owner, ownership can be moved, and values are dropped when the owner goes out of scope.

topics.label: Move Semantics
topics.description: Assignment moves ownership for heap types. After a move, the original variable is invalid.

topics.label: Clone & Copy
topics.description: Explicit deep copies with .clone() and implicit copies for Copy types. Stack-only types implement Copy.

topics.label: References & Borrowing
topics.description: Borrow values with & (shared) and &mut (exclusive). Borrowing avoids ownership transfer.

topics.label: Borrowing Rules
topics.description: Multiple shared references OR one mutable reference, never both. This prevents data races at compile time.

topics.label: Slices
topics.description: Borrow a contiguous section of a collection with &[T] and &str. Slices are references without ownership.

topics.label: Lifetimes Basics
topics.description: Annotate how long references live with 'a. Lifetimes prevent dangling references at compile time.

topics.label: Lifetime Elision
topics.description: The compiler infers lifetimes in common patterns. Understanding elision rules reduces explicit annotations.

core.label: Structs, Enums & Pattern Matching
core.description: Custom types, enums with data, and exhaustive matching.

topics.label: Structs
topics.description: Define named fields, tuple structs, and unit structs. Structs are Rust's primary data modeling tool.

topics.label: Methods & impl
topics.description: Attach methods to structs with impl blocks. Associated functions (no self) serve as constructors.

topics.label: Enums
topics.description: Enums with variants that carry data. Rust enums are algebraic data types — far more powerful than C enums.

topics.label: Option<T>
topics.description: Represent optional values with Some(T) or None. Rust has no null — Option forces explicit handling.

topics.label: Result<T, E>
topics.description: Represent success or failure with Ok(T) or Err(E). Result is Rust's primary error handling mechanism.

topics.label: Pattern Matching (match)
topics.description: Exhaustive matching on enums, structs, and values. match is Rust's most powerful control flow construct.

topics.label: if let & while let
topics.description: Concise pattern matching for single variants. Shorter than match when you only care about one case.

topics.label: The ? Operator
topics.description: Propagate errors concisely with ?. Replaces verbose match/unwrap chains in functions returning Result.

core.label: Traits & Generics
core.description: Trait-based polymorphism, generics, and trait objects.

topics.label: Traits
topics.description: Define shared behavior with trait. Traits are Rust's version of interfaces.

topics.label: Implementing Traits
topics.description: impl Trait for Type. You can implement your traits on any type, or std traits on your types.

topics.label: Derive Macros
topics.description: Auto-implement common traits with #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, etc.)]. Reduces boilerplate.

topics.label: Generics
topics.description: Write type-generic functions and structs with <T>. Generics are monomorphized — zero runtime cost.

topics.label: Trait Bounds
topics.description: Constrain generics with T: Trait and where clauses. Trait bounds specify what operations a generic type supports.

topics.label: Trait Objects (dyn Trait)
topics.description: Dynamic dispatch with Box<dyn Trait>. Trait objects enable runtime polymorphism at the cost of indirection.

topics.label: Common Std Traits
topics.description: Display, Debug, Clone, Default, From/Into, Iterator, Drop. These traits integrate your types with the ecosystem.

topics.label: Associated Types
topics.description: Types defined inside traits (type Item). Used in Iterator and other traits to reduce generic noise.

core.label: Error Handling & Closures
core.description: Error management patterns, closures, and iterators.

topics.label: Error Handling Strategy
topics.description: Use Result for recoverable errors, panic! for unrecoverable. Design error types for your library.

topics.label: Custom Error Types
topics.description: Define error enums implementing std::error::Error. Custom errors provide context for callers.

topics.label: anyhow & thiserror
topics.description: anyhow for applications (easy error context), thiserror for libraries (derive Error). The standard error crates.

topics.label: Closures
topics.description: Anonymous functions with |args| body that capture their environment. Used with iterators and callbacks.

topics.label: Fn, FnMut, FnOnce
topics.description: Closure traits based on how they capture: by reference, mutable reference, or move. Determines closure flexibility.

topics.label: Iterators
topics.description: Lazy, composable data processing with .iter(), .map(), .filter(), .collect(). Iterators are zero-cost abstractions.

topics.label: Iterator Adaptors
topics.description: chain, zip, enumerate, take, skip, flat_map, and peekable. Compose complex data pipelines.

topics.label: Implementing Iterator
topics.description: Create custom iterators for your types. Implement the Iterator trait with type Item and fn next().

core.label: Modules, Crates & Testing
core.description: Project organization, the crate ecosystem, and testing.

topics.label: Modules & Visibility
topics.description: Organize code with mod, pub, and use. Modules control visibility and structure within a crate.

topics.label: Crates & Cargo.toml
topics.description: Manage dependencies, features, and workspaces. crates.io is Rust's package registry.

topics.label: Unit Tests
topics.description: Write tests with #[test] in the same file. Rust's test runner is built into Cargo.

topics.label: Integration Tests
topics.description: Test public APIs from the tests/ directory. Integration tests see your crate as an external user would.

topics.label: Documentation & Doc Tests
topics.description: Write /// doc comments with examples that run as tests. Rust's doc tests keep documentation accurate.

topics.label: Clippy
topics.description: Lint Rust code with cargo clippy. Clippy catches common mistakes and suggests idiomatic improvements.

topics.label: rustfmt
topics.description: Auto-format code with cargo fmt. Consistent formatting across all Rust projects.

topics.label: Workspaces
topics.description: Multi-crate projects with shared dependencies. Workspaces are Rust's monorepo solution.

core.label: Concurrency & Async
core.description: Fearless concurrency, async/await, and smart pointers.

topics.label: Threads
topics.description: Spawn OS threads with std::thread::spawn. Rust's ownership prevents data races at compile time.

topics.label: Channels (mpsc)
topics.description: Send data between threads with channels. Multiple producers, single consumer.

topics.label: Mutex & Arc
topics.description: Shared mutable state with Mutex<T> and thread-safe reference counting with Arc<T>.

topics.label: Send & Sync Traits
topics.description: Marker traits that enable safe concurrency. Send = transferable between threads, Sync = shareable.

topics.label: async/await
topics.description: Asynchronous programming with async fn and .await. Requires an async runtime like Tokio.

topics.label: Tokio Runtime
topics.description: The most popular async runtime. Provides async I/O, timers, channels, and task spawning.

topics.label: Box, Rc, RefCell
topics.description: Smart pointers for heap allocation (Box), reference counting (Rc), and interior mutability (RefCell).

topics.label: Unsafe Rust
topics.description: Bypass compiler checks with unsafe blocks. Required for FFI, raw pointers, and low-level performance.
